Today is February 9, 2026. Ghislaine Maxwell appeared virtually for a closed-door deposition from a Texas prison. According to her lawyer, David Oscar Markus, under what specific condition is Maxwell prepared to "speak fully and honestly"?
(A) If she is granted clemency by President Trump
(B) If she is granted legal immunity by the House Oversight Committee
(C) If the Department of Justice agrees to redact the names of 25 co-conspirators
(D) If she is allowed to meet with Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche again
(E) If the Supreme Court issues a favorable ruling regarding her 20-year sentence
explanation: The article states that Maxwell's lawyer, David Oscar Markus, posted on social media that Maxwell was "prepared to speak fully and honestly if granted clemency by President Trump."
model_output:
On February 9, 2026, Ghislaine Maxwell appeared virtually before the House Oversight Committee for a closed-door deposition from a federal prison in Texas. During the deposition, she repeatedly invoked her Fifth Amendment right against self-incrimination and declined to answer lawmakers' questions. 

Her attorney, David Oscar Markus, read a statement to the committee explaining that Maxwell had to remain silent due to her pending legal petitions. However, Markus explicitly stated that there was a "straightforward path" for the committee and the public to hear the truth: "Ms. Maxwell is prepared to speak fully and honestly if granted clemency by President Trump". He added that she would be able to provide a complete account and testify that both President Donald Trump and former President Bill Clinton are "innocent of any wrongdoing".

Therefore, the specific condition under which she is prepared to speak fully and honestly is if she is granted clemency by President Trump.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [The Washington Post] (https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2026/02/09/ghislaine-maxwell-deposition-epstein/)
- [CBS News] (https://www.cbsnews.com/news/ghislaine-maxwell-pleads-fifth-house-deposition-epstein-probe/)
- [TIME] (https://time.com/7206851/ghislaine-maxwell-clemency-trump-testimony/)

Today is February 9, 2026. The US military recently boarded an oil tanker named Aquila II in the Indian Ocean. According to the Department of Defense, what was the specific reason for tracking and boarding this vessel?
(A) It was suspected of transporting narcotics from the Caribbean to the Indian Ocean
(B) It was operating in defiance of a US "quarantine of sanctioned vessels in the Caribbean"
(C) It was carrying Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro following his removal from power
(D) It was a vessel associated with Chevron that failed to report its cargo to US authorities
(E) It was identified as a "hidden tranche" of information related to the Epstein investigation
explanation: The Department of Defense stated the ship was operating in defiance of a US "quarantine of sanctioned vessels in the Caribbean."
model_output:
On February 9, 2026, the US military intercepted and boarded the sanctioned oil tanker *Aquila II* in the Indian Ocean. According to statements from the Department of Defense (referred to in some recent communications as the Department of War), the vessel was tracked and hunted across multiple ocean basins because it was operating in defiance of a US-imposed "quarantine of sanctioned vessels in the Caribbean". The tanker had fled the Caribbean region following the US military's operations in Venezuela and was pursued by US forces until it was boarded in the Indo-Pacific Command's area of responsibility. 

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- Wikipedia: United States oil blockade during Operation Southern Spear (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States_oil_blockade_during_Operation_Southern_Spear)
- gCaptain: U.S. Boards Aquila II Tanker in Major Operation (https://gcaptain.com/u-s-boards-aquila-ii-tanker-in-major-operation/)
- United24 Media: “It Ran, We Followed”: US Tracks and Boards Sanctioned Aquila II Vessel Across Oceans (https://united24media.com/latest-news/it-ran-we-followed-us-tracks-and-boards-sanctioned-aquila-ii-vessel-across-oceans-5421)

Today is February 9, 2026. The Seattle Seahawks won Super Bowl 60 on Sunday. What specific medical detail is provided regarding the birth of Seahawks linebacker Derick Hall?
(A) He was born weighing 6lb 3in and had a 5% chance of survival
(B) He was born three months premature and weighed exactly 3lb
(C) He weighed less than 3lb and was on life support when his mother refused to have it discontinued
(D) He was born in Santa Clara, California, with a 1% chance of survival
(E) He was born weighing 18st 2lb, which required immediate life support
explanation: The article mentions that Derick Hall was born four months premature in 2001, weighed less than 3lb, and was on life support when his mother refused to have it discontinued.
model_output:
Based on the medical details surrounding his birth, **Derick Hall** was born four months premature in Gulfport, Mississippi, weighing just 2 pounds and 1 ounce (with some sources citing 2 pounds, 9 ounces). Because he was born without a heartbeat and suffered from brain bleeding, he was placed on life support. Doctors believed he would likely remain in a permanent vegetative state and advised his mother, Stacy Gooden-Crandle, to allow life support to be discontinued, but she refused and fought for his life. 

This aligns perfectly with option (C).

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [1] Wikipedia: Derick Hall (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Derick_Hall)
- [2] Derick Hall One Percent Foundation (https://www.dhallonepercent.org/about-derick-hall)

Today is February 9, 2026. A group of Jeffrey Epstein survivors released a video calling for the release of more files. According to the text slate in the advert, on what specific date was the Epstein Files Transparency Act signed into law?
(A) December 19, 2025
(B) January 30, 2026
(C) February 1, 2026
(D) November 19, 2025
(E) August 15, 2025
explanation: The advert featuring the survivors began with a text slate on screen that said: "On November 19, 2025, the Epstein Files Transparency Act was signed into law."
model_output:
To determine the date the Epstein Files Transparency Act was signed into law, we can look at the legislative history of the bill. 

The Epstein Files Transparency Act (H.R. 4405) was passed by the U.S. House of Representatives on November 18, 2025, and unanimously approved by the U.S. Senate the following day. It was officially signed into law by President Donald Trump on **November 19, 2025**. 

The survivor-led video, which aired during the Super Bowl in February 2026 to demand further transparency and the unredacted release of the remaining documents, references this specific signing date on its text slate.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- Wikipedia: Epstein Files Transparency Act (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Epstein_Files_Transparency_Act)
- Department of Justice: Department of Justice Publishes 3.5 Million Responsive Pages in Compliance with the Epstein Files Transparency Act (https://www.justice.gov/opa/pr/department-justice-publishes-35-million-responsive-pages-compliance-epstein-files)
- The White House: Congressional Bill H.R. 4405 Signed into Law (https://www.whitehouse.gov/briefing-room/legislation/2025/11/19/press-release-bill-signed-hr-4405/)

Today is February 9, 2026. White House aide Stephen Miller has faced criticism following the shooting of Alex Pretti in Minneapolis. What was Miller's rare "walk-back" statement regarding the administration's initial assessment of the incident?
(A) He admitted that Pretti was a licensed handgun owner and not a domestic terrorist
(B) He stated that the Customs and Border Patrol officials had acted in self-defense after being threatened
(C) He claimed that the video evidence of the shooting had been manipulated by Democratic critics
(D) He announced that the administration would now adopt a "softer touch" in immigration enforcement
(E) He contended that the initial assessment was based on reports from "on the ground" officers who may not have been following proper protocol
explanation: Miller provided a statement to the media contending that the administration's initial assessment was "based on reports" from "on the ground" immigration officers—and that those agents "may not have been following" proper protocol.
model_output:
Following the fatal shooting of Alex Pretti in Minneapolis, White House aide Stephen Miller initially labeled Pretti a "would-be assassin" and a "domestic terrorist." However, as bystander videos emerged contradicting the administration's narrative, Miller issued a rare "walk-back" statement. 

In his statement, Miller shifted the blame to the agents involved, claiming that the administration's initial assessment was "based on reports from CBP on the ground". He further conceded that the Customs and Border Protection (CBP) team "may not have been following" proper protocol, which had been established to create a physical barrier between arrest teams and protesters. This marked a significant reversal for Miller, who is typically known for doubling down on his hardline positions.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- The Guardian (https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/jan/28/stephen-miller-alex-pretti-killing-trump)
- KESQ (https://kesq.com/news/2026/01/27/top-white-house-aide-stephen-miller-acknowledges-possible-breach-of-protocol-before-alex-prettis-shooting/)
- Steve Harvey FM (https://steveharveyfm.com/content/2026-01-28-stephen-miller-makes-contradicting-admission-about-alex-pretti-shooting/)

Today is February 9, 2026. Several AI companies ran advertisements during the Super Bowl. What was the specific slogan used at the end of the commercial for the AI assistant Claude?
(A) "Ads are coming to AI. But not to Claude."
(B) "The only thing more powerful than hate is love."
(C) "Together, We Are America."
(D) "Claude: The AI that doesn't plot against you."
(E) "Free your mind from the hidden tranche of information."
explanation: The article states that the Anthropic Super Bowl ad for Claude ends with the slogan: "Ads are coming to AI. But not to Claude."
model_output:
During Super Bowl LX in February 2026, the AI company Anthropic ran a series of commercials for its AI assistant, Claude. The ads took a direct shot at rival OpenAI's decision to introduce advertisements into ChatGPT. The commercials featured scenarios where people asked an AI persona for help with personal issues or fitness, only for the AI to abruptly pivot into a jarring, sponsored product pitch. 

At the end of these commercials, the specific slogan displayed on-screen was: **"Ads are coming to AI. But not to Claude."**

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- ispot.tv (https://www.ispot.tv/ad/6123/anthropic-super-bowl-2026-betrayal-song-by-dr-dre)
- Business Insider (https://www.businessinsider.com/anthropic-skewered-openai-won-ai-super-bowl-2026-2)
- Mashable (https://mashable.com/article/anthropic-super-bowl-lx-ads-chatgpt)

Today is February 9, 2026. US news anchor Savannah Guthrie has pleaded for the return of her mother, Nancy Guthrie. What specific detail did authorities release regarding Nancy Guthrie's pacemaker?
(A) The pacemaker itself was found disconnected and removed from her home
(B) The app connected to the device was disconnected from her phone at about 02:28 local time
(C) The device sent a distress signal to the Pima County sheriff's office at 02:28 local time
(D) The pacemaker was found to be missing its necessary life-saving medication
(E) The device was used by the FBI to track her location to a house in Tucson
explanation: Authorities stated that at about 02:28 local time on the morning she went missing, "the app connected to her pacemaker, an implanted cardiac device, was disconnected from her phone."
model_output:
In the investigation into the disappearance of Nancy Guthrie, mother of *Today* show anchor Savannah Guthrie, authorities released a detailed timeline of the events from the night she went missing. 

According to the Pima County Sheriff's Department, one of the crucial digital clues was that the Bluetooth connection between Nancy Guthrie's implanted pacemaker and the monitoring app on her smartphone was severed at exactly 2:28 a.m. local time. Because her phone was left behind at her home, the disconnection indicated that she (and the pacemaker inside her) had been moved out of the device's Bluetooth range at that specific time. 

Authorities clarified that pacemakers do not have GPS tracking capabilities and cannot send distress signals to law enforcement. Furthermore, the pacemaker itself remains inside her and continues to function autonomously without the phone connection. 

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [PBS News] (https://www.pbs.org/newshour/nation/a-timeline-of-the-disappearance-of-and-search-for-nancy-guthrie)
- [The Washington Post] (https://www.washingtonpost.com/nation/2026/02/19/nancy-guthrie-pacemaker-search/)
- [Forbes] (https://www.forbes.com/sites/robertglatter/2026/02/06/can-nancy-guthries-pacemaker-offer-clues-about-her-disappearance/)

Today is February 9, 2026. Bad Bunny performed at the Super Bowl in a beige sweater emblazoned with the number 64. According to the article, what is the potential significance of this number?
(A) It represents the number of Spanish-speaking nations in the Americas
(B) It is the number of Grammy awards Bad Bunny has won in his career
(C) It could represent the official death toll of the 2017 Hurricane Maria
(D) It represents the number of days Liam Conejo Ramos was detained by ICE
(E) It is the street number of the "casita" where Bad Bunny grew up in Puerto Rico
explanation: The article notes that the number 64 "could represent the official death toll [of Hurricane Maria], which turned out to be significantly lower than the estimated thousands who died."
model_output:
Based on the details from the articles discussing Bad Bunny's 2026 Super Bowl halftime show, the number 64 on his custom "Ocasio 64" sweater sparked multiple interpretations. While Bad Bunny later confirmed it was a tribute to his late uncle's birth year (1964), a prominent political interpretation that emerged immediately following the performance was that **it could represent the initial official death toll of the 2017 Hurricane Maria**. 

As pointed out by journalists analyzing the performance's politically charged imagery (such as dancers suspended from sparking power poles, referencing the island's infrastructure collapse), the Puerto Rican government initially reported the Hurricane Maria death toll as 64—a heavily disputed figure that was later revealed to be dramatically underestimated. 

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [Mashable] (https://mashable.com/article/bad-bunny-super-bowl-halftime-show-jersey-meaning)
- [San Francisco Chronicle] (https://www.sfchronicle.com/entertainment/article/bad-bunny-super-bowl-jersey-18656789.php)

(A) Olimpia delle Tofane Hospital in the Dolomites
(B) Cortina Medical Center in Italy
(C) St. Moritz Clinic in Switzerland
(D) Ca Foncello hospital in Treviso
(E) Vancouver General Hospital in Canada
explanation: The article states: "Vonn was airlifted off the piste to Ca Foncello hospital in Treviso, around 130km from Olimpia delle Tofane in the Dolomites."
model_output:
On February 8, 2026, during the women's downhill event at the Milano Cortina Winter Olympics, American skier Lindsey Vonn suffered a severe crash just seconds into her run. Following the accident, she was airlifted from the course in Cortina d'Ampezzo and transported to **Ca' Foncello Hospital** in the city of Treviso, Italy. There, she underwent multiple surgeries to stabilize a complex fracture in her left leg. 

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

Based on the provided context for the 2025/2026 Canadian TV adaptation of *Heated Rivalry*, the show's fifth and penultimate episode, titled "I'll Believe in Anything," achieved massive critical acclaim. 

Specifically, it set a major IMDb record by scoring a perfect 10/10 rating from thousands of reviewers. This feat tied it with the legendary *Breaking Bad* episode "Ozymandias" for the highest-rated television episode of all time on the platform.

(A)

Today is February 9, 2026. The Seattle Seahawks logo was inspired by a ceremonial mask. From which specific tribe and location was this mask's inspiration traced?
(A) The Coast Salish tribe near Tacoma, Washington
(B) The Kwakwaka'wakw tribe on Vancouver Island
(C) The Puyallup Tribe in Seattle, Washington
(D) The Hudson Nation in Maine
(E) The Port Alberni community in British Columbia
explanation: The article states that the "Seahawks logo was copied from the Kwakwaka'wakw tribe on Vancouver Island."
model_output:
The Seattle Seahawks logo was inspired by a Kwakwaka'wakw transformation mask. When the NFL commissioned the logo in 1976, the designers referenced Robert Bruce Inverarity's 1950 book, *Art of the Northwest Coast Indians*, which featured a photograph of this specific ceremonial mask. 

The mask was created by the Kwakwaka'wakw (pronounced KWA-kwuh-kyuh-wakw) people, an Indigenous group from the northeastern part of Vancouver Island in British Columbia, Canada. Although Seattle itself is located on traditional Coast Salish territory, the designers utilized the northern Northwest Coast art style of the Kwakwaka'wakw for the team's iconic raptor profile. 

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- CBC News: "How this Indigenous mask from B.C. inspired the Seattle Seahawks logo" (https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/british-columbia/seahawks-logo-indigenous-mask-1.6715555)
- Tulalip News: "From inspiration to artistic reimagining, Seahawks logo embraced in Coast Salish territory" (https://www.tulalipnews.com/wp/2021/01/28/from-inspiration-to-artistic-reimagining-seahawks-logo-embraced-in-coast-salish-territory/)
- Burke Museum: "The mask that inspired the Seahawks logo" (https://www.burkemuseum.org/news/mask-inspired-seahawks-logo)

(C) That a quinceañera is the celebration of a girl's 15th birthday, not 16th

(E) The name of the conservative organization, which is Turning Point USA, not Turning Point America
explanation: The correction note states: "An earlier version of this article incorrectly said a quinceañera is the celebration of a girl's 16th birthday party. This was later updated to say it is the celebration of a girl's 15th birthday."
model_output:
To determine the correct answer, we can look at the context of the February 7, 2026, Washington Post article titled *"The enthusiasm — and scorn — surrounding Bad Bunny's star turn at the Super Bowl."* 

In the piece, the authors discuss how Latino fans were preparing for Bad Bunny's highly anticipated Super Bowl LX halftime show. The original publication of the article contained a cultural gaffe regarding a fan who was hosting a viewing party and likened the massive event to a "quinceañera." 

The initial text incorrectly defined a quinceañera as a "traditional Latin American 16th birthday celebration" (conflating the milestone with an American "Sweet 16"). Because *quince* translates to fifteen in Spanish, a quinceañera is specifically a celebration of a girl's 15th birthday. The Washington Post quickly updated the text to remove the incorrect age and appended a correction note at the bottom of the article to clarify the factual error. 

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- The Washington Post ("The enthusiasm — and scorn — surrounding Bad Bunny's star turn at the Super Bowl", February 7, 2026) (https://www.washingtonpost.com)

Today is February 9, 2026. President Donald Trump has proposed the "Great Healthcare Plan." According to the article, what is one of the specific pledges included in this plan?
(A) To provide government-funded insurance for all citizens over the age of 66
(B) To renew the Covid subsidies that expired last year for private insurance
(C) To ban medical debt from being included in credit reports nationwide
(D) To remove "kickbacks" to middlemen
(E) To consolidate Medicare, Medicaid, and the marketplace into a single system
explanation: The article states: "The plan includes a pledge to remove 'kickbacks' to middlemen."
model_output:
On January 15, 2026, President Donald Trump announced his "Great Healthcare Plan," a legislative framework aimed at lowering healthcare costs, increasing price transparency, and holding insurance companies accountable. 

Among the specific pledges outlined in the plan's framework is a proposal to lower insurance premiums by ending the "kickbacks" paid by pharmacy benefit managers (PBMs) to large brokerage middlemen. The administration argues that these kickbacks deceptively raise the cost of health insurance for Americans. 

Other key components of the plan include codifying "most-favored-nation" drug pricing, funding a cost-sharing reduction program, and requiring insurers and providers to publish their prices and coverage comparisons in "plain English". The plan does not propose government-funded insurance for all citizens over 66, renewing the expired Covid-era enhanced ACA subsidies (it actually proposes redirecting those funds directly to consumers), banning medical debt from credit reports, or consolidating Medicare and Medicaid into a single system.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- Committee for a Responsible Federal Budget (https://www.crfb.org/blogs/white-house-releases-great-healthcare-plan)
- Fox Business (https://www.foxbusiness.com/politics/trump-administration-targets-pharmacy-benefit-manager-kickbacks-lower-drug-costs)
- The White House (https://www.whitehouse.gov/briefing-room/statements-releases/2026/01/15/fact-sheet-president-donald-j-trump-calls-on-congress-to-enact-the-great-healthcare-plan/)

(C) He has won 10 Super Bowls as a coach, including those with Tom Brady
(D) He made the infamous decision to throw on the one-yard line 11 years ago

explanation: The article states: "Mike Vrabel can become the first man to win a Super Bowl as head coach and player with the same team."
model_output:
Based on the provided context of Super Bowl 60 in 2026, Mike Vrabel is the head coach of the New England Patriots. The historical detail mentioned regarding him is that with a victory over the Seattle Seahawks, he could make NFL history by becoming the first person to win a Super Bowl as both a player and a head coach for the same franchise. Vrabel previously won three Super Bowl rings as a linebacker for the Patriots during the 2001, 2003, and 2004 seasons. 

The other options refer to different figures or incorrect facts:
* **(B)** refers to Seattle Seahawks head coach Mike Macdonald, who was previously the defensive coordinator for the Baltimore Ravens.
* **(D)** refers to former Seahawks head coach Pete Carroll, who made the infamous play-call at the one-yard line against the Patriots in Super Bowl XLIX (11 years prior to Super Bowl 60).

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

(A) Due to a request from Maxwell's lawyers to wait for a Supreme Court ruling related to her case
(B) Because the Department of Justice had not yet released the three million pages of files
(C) Because Maxwell had not yet met with Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche
(D) Due to the passage of the Epstein Files Transparency Act in November
(E) Because the House Oversight Committee was waiting for the results of the 2025 midterm elections
explanation: The article states: "Monday's deposition was originally scheduled for last August but was postponed by Comer after a request from Maxwell's lawyers to wait for a Supreme Court ruling related to her case."
model_output:
In August 2025, House Oversight Committee Chairman James Comer originally scheduled a deposition with Ghislaine Maxwell for August 11. However, Maxwell's legal team responded to the subpoena with a list of conditions, which included a request to delay her testimony until the Supreme Court resolved her pending appeal regarding her sex trafficking conviction. 

Chairman Comer agreed to this request, stating that the committee was willing to postpone the deposition until after the Supreme Court reviewed her case and issued a ruling [1, 2]. The deposition was eventually rescheduled and took place on February 9, 2026, where Maxwell invoked her Fifth Amendment rights.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

- CBS News (https://www.cbsnews.com)
- Axios (https://www.axios.com)

(C) He became the first overseas coach to win a Super Bowl
(D) He became the first coach to win a Super Bowl after starting in the Canadian league
(E) He became the first coach to win a Super Bowl while also serving as a player
explanation: The article states: "Seattle defensive coordinator Aden Durde became the first overseas coach to win a Super Bowl, after beginning his coaching journey in the British amateur game."
model_output:
On February 8, 2026, the Seattle Seahawks defeated the New England Patriots in Super Bowl LX (60). With this victory, Seattle's defensive coordinator Aden Durde, who was born in Middlesex, England, made history by becoming the first overseas (British-born) coach to win a Super Bowl. 

Durde's journey to the NFL is a unique one; he played in the British American Football League and NFL Europe before transitioning to coaching with the London Warriors. He eventually worked his way up through the NFL ranks, becoming the league's first full-time British coach in 2018 with the Atlanta Falcons, and later the first British-born defensive coordinator when hired by the Seahawks in 2024. 

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

(A) The seizure of the Aquila II tanker in the Indian Ocean
(B) The acquisition of Greenland from the Danish government
(C) The military operation to remove Nicolas Maduro from power in Venezuela
(D) The destruction of boats suspected of narcotics trafficking in the Caribbean
(E) Airstrikes on Houthi rebels in Yemen
explanation: The article states: "Last March... a series of Signal text messages... revealed Miller at the centre of White House planning... 'As I heard it, the president was clear: green light,' he wrote to the group of senior aides... preparing to launch airstrikes on Houthi rebels in Yemen."
model_output:
Based on the leaked Signal text messages published by *The Atlantic* in March 2025 (an incident widely referred to as "Signalgate"), Stephen Miller confirmed that President Donald Trump had given a "green light" for **airstrikes on Houthi rebels in Yemen**. 

In the group chat, which mistakenly included *The Atlantic*'s editor-in-chief Jeffrey Goldberg, top administration officials were debating the timing and implications of the military strikes. When Vice President JD Vance and others expressed reservations about the operation and "bailing Europe out," Miller effectively ended the debate by stating, "As I heard it, the president was clear: green light, but we soon make clear to Egypt and Europe what we expect in return".

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

- theguardian.com (https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2025/jun/15/stephen-miller-trump-immigration-policy)

model_output:

</final_answer>
